title: Session display Phase 3 ‚Äî Detach (detachable windows)
body:
## Requirement

**Session display Phase 3 ‚Äî Detach: detachable session windows.** Move a session into its own OS window (draggable to a 2nd monitor), session keeps running; reattach back into the main window.

- **Legacy stays the default** (non-breaking); detach is an **action**, not a third exclusive mode. Controlled via setting `global.sessionDisplayMode`.
- Builds on **Phase 1 tabs (#6)** + **Phase 2 settings window (#7)** (both done) ‚Äî uses the multi-window pattern proven there.
- Central change: route `terminal-data` per session to the owning window (sidebar/attention events stay in the main window).

Detailed design and implementation plan ‚Üí comment.

title: Review 2026-07: main-process sync IO and redundant reads on hot paths
body:
# Main-process performance: synchronous full-file IO and redundant reads on hot paths

Cluster from the 2026-07-05 multi-agent code review. Line numbers pin to commit `2a10383`. **Verify every finding against the current code before fixing** ‚Äî if a finding does not reproduce, reject it with a one-line reason instead of forcing a change.

These findings block or stall the Electron main process (event-loop freezes, UI stalls) or waste IO on hot paths.

## Findings

- `session-cache.js:302` + `read-session-file.js:147` ‚Äî **high** ‚Äî the `refreshFile` hot path re-reads the complete session JSONL synchronously on every debounce flush (up to every 3 s per busy session). Large transcripts (100+ MB) freeze the event loop. `readSessionDisplayHeader` (`read-session-file.js:336`) is dead code even though a comment near `main.js:1153` claims a header-only read is used. Fix: use the header-only read + merge with the cached row on the incremental path.
- `main.js:2423` ‚Äî **high** ‚Äî `read-session-jsonl` (transcript viewer open) reads and `JSON.parse`s the whole transcript synchronously on the main thread. Fix: move to a worker or stream/async read.
- `shell-profiles.js:59` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî `execSync('wsl.exe --list --quiet', { timeout: 5000 })` blocks the main thread up to 5 s on the first `getShellProfiles()`/`resolveShell()` call (WSL utility-VM cold start is really that slow). Fix: async discovery (`execFile`) or lazy background warm-up.
- `session-cache.js:361` + `folder-index-state.js:16-23` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî the reconcile pass calls `getFolderIndexMtimeMs` per folder: readdir + `statSync` per `.jsonl`. Hundreds of ms of sync IO per pass with thousands of sessions. Fix: cache per flush or move to the scan worker.
- `main.js:1216` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî `get-plans` reindexes FTS on every call (no `shouldReindex` gate like memory/work-file) and reads each plan file twice (title at :1205, body at :1221). Fix: reuse the signature gate, read content once.
- `main.js:1426` + `main.js:1550` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî `get-memories`: `scanMdFiles` reads each `.md` fully just for the emptiness check, then the FTS block reads the same files again. Fix: `stat.size > 0` instead of a content read; cache content once.
- `session-cache.js:153` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî `refreshFolder` does a linear scan over `cachedMap` per file (filePath match) ‚Äî O(files √ó cached) per watcher flush. Fix: build a reverse map `filePath ‚Üí entry` once.
- `main.js:802-810` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `get-projects-admin` parses the ~160 KB `~/.claude.json` three times per call (trust map, meta map, raw read). Fix: read once, derive all three.
- `db.js:1055` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `isSearchIndexPopulated` calls `db.prepare()` on every invocation (runs in every `get-projects`). Fix: memoize the statement like the others.
- `main.js:753` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `remap-project` rewrites all JSONLs synchronously inside the IPC handler, and matches `parsed.cwd === oldPath` exactly (misses separator/case variants). Rarely used, but blocking.

## Acceptance

- Each finding fixed or explicitly rejected (one-line reason in an issue comment).
- `npm test` green, no new failures.
- No behavior change beyond latency/blocking improvements.

title: Review 2026-07: renderer correctness ‚Äî listener leaks, races, state bugs
body:
# Renderer correctness: listener leaks, races, and state bugs

Cluster from the 2026-07-05 multi-agent code review. Line numbers pin to commit `2a10383`. **Verify every finding against the current code before fixing** ‚Äî reject non-reproducible findings with a one-line reason.

## Findings

- `public/dialogs.js:362-371` and `:485-493` ‚Äî **high** ‚Äî `showNewSessionDialog`/`showResumeSessionDialog` register a `document` `keydown` listener that is only removed on Esc/Enter, never in `close()` (cancel, overlay click, start button). Every opened dialog leaks a listener; a later Enter (outside an input) calls `start()`/`resume()` on the detached dialog ‚Üí ghost session launch per leaked listener. Fix: `document.removeEventListener('keydown', onKey)` in `close()` (`showAddProjectDialog:527-530` does it correctly).
- `public/plans-memory-view.js:84-90`, `:281-286`, `:433-439` ‚Äî **high** ‚Äî `openPlan`/`openMemory`/`openWorkFile` each hide only a hand-maintained subset of viewers instead of calling the existing `hideAllViewers()` (line 95) ‚Üí overlapping views are possible, and `drainViewerWatches()` is skipped so `fs.watchFile` polls from the JSONL viewer keep running forever (exactly what the comment at lines 106-110 tries to prevent). Fix: call `hideAllViewers()` first in all three openers.
- `public/stats-view.js:201`, `:336`, `:390` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî local midnight ‚Üí `toISOString().slice(0,10)` yields the UTC date; in TZ+1/+2 the heatmap, 30-day, and streak buckets shift one day vs. the DB date keys (known UTC pitfall in this project). Fix: local `YYYY-MM-DD` formatting.
- `public/app.js:1663-1744` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî `loadProjects` has no in-flight guard; overlapping calls (watcher debounce, tab switch, favorite/archive handlers) can resolve out of order ‚Äî the slower, older response overwrites the fresher `cachedProjects`. Fix: generation counter, drop stale responses.
- `public/session-tabs.js:74-79` + `public/app.js:2239-2246` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî read-modify-write of the whole `global` settings blob (tab drag / sidebar resize) without serialization ‚Äî races with settings save or a second window can clobber unrelated settings. Fix: serialize through one queue or use key-scoped setters.
- `public/settings-panel.js:1244-1249` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `settings.notifications` merges over the `current.notifications` frozen at open time; changes from a second settings window since then are lost on save.
- `public/app.js:956-960` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `onSessionForked` sets `pendingEntry.sessionId`, a field that does not exist in the pending shape `{ session, projectPath, folder }`; works only because `entry.session` is the same object. Correct: `pendingEntry.session.sessionId`.
- `public/viewer-panel.js:107-109` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî the `onFileChanged` IPC listener registered in the constructor is never removed in `destroy()` (line 351). All four panels are singletons today, but any further instantiation leaks a listener.
- `public/stats-view.js:6-66` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `loadStats()` without a generation token: two overlapping calls (tab double-click) can resolve out of order ‚Üí stale result wins. Fix: gen counter like viewer-panel `_openGen`.
- `public/grid-view.js:211-216` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî undo of "Mark ready seen" restores only sessions whose PTY is still active (`if (activePtyIds.has(sid))`) ‚Äî undo is silently incomplete.

## Acceptance

- Each finding fixed or explicitly rejected (one-line reason in an issue comment).
- `npm test` green. Manual spot-check: open/cancel the New Session dialog repeatedly, then press Enter outside any input ‚Äî no session must launch.

title: Review 2026-07: main-process robustness ‚Äî lifecycle races, broken invalidation, platform bugs
body:
# Main-process robustness: lifecycle races, broken invalidation, platform bugs

Cluster from the 2026-07-05 multi-agent code review. Line numbers pin to commit `2a10383`. **Verify every finding against the current code before fixing** ‚Äî reject non-reproducible findings with a one-line reason.

## Findings

- `main.js:747` ‚Äî **high** ‚Äî `remap-project` builds the folder key with a local `oldPath.replace(/[/_]/g,'-')` instead of `encodeProjectPath()` (which replaces ALL non-alphanumerics incl. `\`, `.`, space). On Windows paths this produces the wrong key ‚Üí "No session data found". Fix: use `encodeProjectPath(oldPath)`.
- `main.js:2246` + `shell-profiles.js:104-108` ‚Äî **high** ‚Äî `_shellProfiles = null` writes an implicit global in main.js; the real cache lives privately in shell-profiles.js and is never invalidated ‚Üí "refresh on each request" is a no-op; newly installed shells only appear after an app restart. Fix: export an `invalidateShellProfiles()` from shell-profiles.js and call it.
- `workers/search-query.js:49-56` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî if better-sqlite3/DB fails to load, the worker stays "online" and answers everything with `[]` forever. Main believes search works and never uses the working sync fallback `searchByType` ‚Üí search silently dead. Fix: exit non-zero; the circuit breaker in `search-worker-client.js` (max 5 restarts, backoff) then falls back to sync search.
- `search-worker-client.js:146-153` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî `shutdown()` neither drains pending promises (in-flight `window.api.search()` hangs forever at quit) nor cancels a scheduled restart `setTimeout` from `exit` (line 120), which can spawn a new worker **after** `closeDb()`. Fix: drain + clear timer/set shutdown flag.
- `shell-profiles.js:179` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî cmd.exe quoting corrupts values: `^`-escapes inside double quotes are literal in cmd (`a&b` arrives as `a^&b`), `\"` is not a cmd escape, and `%VAR%` expansion inside quotes cannot be suppressed with `^` (secrets/prompts containing `%` get expanded). Triggers whenever `resolveShell` falls back to COMSPEC. Fix: inside quotes only double `"` ‚Üí `""`; handle `%` explicitly; no `^` in quoted parts; add tests.
- `session-transitions.js:141` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî `readNewSessionSignals`: `JSON.parse(line)` without per-line try/catch; the fixed 512 KB read almost always truncates the last line ‚Üí the throw discards all signals already found (forkedFrom etc.) ‚Üí fork/clear detection can permanently fail. Fix: per-line try/catch like `readSessionFile`.
- `mcp-bridge.js:29` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî `findFreePort` probes a port, releases it, then `WebSocketServer` binds later ‚Üí TOCTOU race; also `startMcpServer` resolves before the `listening` event. Fix: `new WebSocketServer({port: 0})` and read the port from `wss.address()`.
- `session-cache.js:637` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî the scan worker is never terminated on quit; `will-quit` closes the DB (`main.js:3263`), and a worker `message` arriving after that writes to the closed DB ‚Üí uncaught exception at shutdown. Fix: keep the worker handle, `terminate()` in will-quit.
- `main.js:2503` + `main.js:2513` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `fs.unwatchFile(filePath)` without the listener reference removes ALL listeners for that file ‚Üí two parallel watches on the same subagent kill each other; listener leak if the renderer never calls stop. Fix: store the listener fn and pass it.
- `main.js:1109` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `save-file-for-panel` checks only `'/.work-files/'` (not `\.work-files\` like line 1707) ‚Üí FTS invalidation never fires on Windows. Fix: same dual check as `read-work-file`.
- `trigger-watcher.js:862` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `start()` performs no initial scan of the trigger directory ‚Äî trigger files dropped while the app was closed are never processed. Fix: one `readdirSync` + dispatch after `fs.watch` setup.
- `mcp-bridge.js:195` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `pendingDiffs` promise has no timeout: if the renderer never answers (reload/crash), the CLI `tools/call` hangs until session end. Fix: timeout with `DIFF_REJECTED` fallback.
- `main.js:2088` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `startAttentionHookServer` guard (`if (attentionHookServer) return`) is only set in the `listen` callback ‚Üí a double call while binding leaks a second server. Fix: set the guard variable immediately.
- `main.js:214` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `settings-changed` is forwarded only to `mainWindow` ‚Äî changes made in the main window never reach the settings popout (only the reverse works). Fix: broadcast to both windows.
- `search-worker-client.js:130-139` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî no per-query timeout: a hung worker keeps the promise open and all subsequent queries queue up. Fix: ~5 s timeout resolving `[]`.
- `shell-profiles.js:149-156` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `windowsToWslPath` cannot handle UNC paths (`\\server\share`) ‚Üí wrong WSL path. Fix: handle/pass through UNC, document.
- `schedule-ipc.js:113-119` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî the creator template is only written when the file is missing ‚Äî after app updates a stale `create-switchboard-schedule.md` persists forever. Fix: version marker in frontmatter, overwrite on change (respect user edits).
- `claude-auth.js:47` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî missing `.credentials.json` logs an error on every usage poll (constant electron-log spam on Win/Linux without login). Fix: treat ENOENT silently.
- `claude-auth.js:128` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî user agent `claude-code/2.1.74` hardcoded; drifts from the real CLI. Fix: central constant + update note, optionally detect installed CLI version.

## Acceptance

- Each finding fixed or explicitly rejected (one-line reason in an issue comment).
- `npm test` green; add tests where the fix is pure logic (cmd quoting, session-transitions parsing).

title: Review 2026-07: security hardening of local attack surface
body:
# Security hardening: local attack surface (hook server, path guards, file loads)

Cluster from the 2026-07-05 multi-agent code review. Line numbers pin to commit `2a10383`. **Verify every finding against the current code before fixing** ‚Äî reject non-reproducible findings with a one-line reason. All items are local-attack-surface hardening (defense in depth for a desktop app), not remote vulnerabilities.

## Findings

- `main.js:2090` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî the attention-hook HTTP server has no authentication (no token in path or header): any local process can forge attention signals and, via `session_id`, trigger the undebounced `refreshFile(immediate)` (line 2115) ‚Üí local DoS through forced full reads. Fix: random token in the hook URL, verify on POST.
- `schedule-ipc.js:191-194` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî path validation of `run-schedule-now` only checks basename `schedule-*.md` + parent dir name `commands`, not that `.claude` sits above it. A compromised renderer can execute any file `X/commands/schedule-*.md` (incl. `~/.claude/commands/`) with arbitrary frontmatter (allowed-tools, permission-mode); `projectPath` then becomes e.g. `~`. Fix: additionally require `path.basename(dotClaudeDir) === '.claude'`, optionally whitelist against known project paths.
- `main.js:1247` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî `save-plan`: `resolved.startsWith(PLANS_DIR)` without a `path.sep` boundary ‚Üí `~/.claude/plansXYZ/...` passes the guard. Fix: check the `PLANS_DIR + path.sep` prefix (pattern exists in `isAllowedMemoryPath`).
- `main.js:1707` + `main.js:1727` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî read/delete-work-file containment = substring `.work-files` anywhere in the path ‚Üí files under arbitrary `.work-files` directories (also outside known projects) are readable/deletable. Fix: validate against known project roots.
- `public/jsonl-viewer.js:649-655` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `[Image: source: <path>]` in transcript text loads arbitrary local paths via `file://` into the DOM ‚Äî transcript-controlled (agent-processed third-party content). Fix: restrict to whitelisted directories / existence check.

## Acceptance

- Each finding fixed or explicitly rejected (one-line reason in an issue comment).
- `npm test` green. The `run-schedule-now` validation should be extracted into a pure function with unit tests (see also the test-gap issue).

title: orphaned hidden projects
body:
## Requirement

**Bug: orphaned hidden projects** ‚Äî `hiddenProjects` (switchboard DB setting) is **not** cleaned up when a project is removed (project management / from Claude); `get-hidden-projects` (`main.js:611`) returns the raw list without reconciling against `.claude.json`/existence ‚Üí the add-project hidden list shows removed projects (verified: several already-removed projects kept showing up, none still in `.claude.json`). Fix: prune `hiddenProjects` on project remove **and** filter `get-hidden-projects` against `.claude.json`. Files: `main.js`.

title: Doubled working-status indicator (ghost line) only heals on resize
body:
Claude's working-status line (e.g. `‚ú≥ Boogieing‚Ä¶`) sporadically renders **twice**: the
upper copy stays frozen while the lower copy keeps updating. A window resize clears the
duplicate and everything looks correct again.

Reproduces during normal session streaming (no resize needed to trigger it). Seen on
Windows / ConPTY sessions.

The indicator should never appear duplicated ‚Äî a single, live-updating status line only.

title: Scope-filtered bookmark views (session / project / global)
body:
Bookmarks need scope-aware views.

**View messages bookmark list**
- Default to the **current session's** bookmarks.
- A scope switcher at the top with three levels: **Session / Project / Global**.

**Project bookmark view**
- A separate **bookmarks icon in the project toolbar** (next to the tasks icon) opens a **large bookmark view** for that project (same pattern as the task view).

Both views support jumping to the bookmarked message and removing a bookmark.

title: Review 2026-07: UX consistency ‚Äî confirmations, error feedback, dialog patterns
body:
# UX consistency: confirmations, error feedback, dialog patterns

Cluster from the 2026-07-05 multi-agent code review. Line numbers pin to commit `2a10383`. **Verify every finding against the current code before fixing** ‚Äî reject non-reproducible findings with a one-line reason. Goal: one consistent pattern per action class (destructive ‚Üí confirm or undo; failure ‚Üí visible feedback; app dialogs, never native `alert`/`confirm`).

## Findings

- `public/dialogs.js:212-217` and `:75-80` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî `launchTerminalSession`/`launchScheduleCreator`: when `openTerminal` fails, the error is written only into the (invisible) terminal and `showSession()` is not called ‚Üí the user never sees the failure. `launchNewSession` (app.js:1793-1797) shows it correctly.
- `public/grid-view.js:237-241` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî bulk "Stop N running" does not mark sessions as user-stopped (`window._markUserStopped`), unlike `confirmAndStopSession` (app.js:1494) ‚Üí misleading "re-click to relaunch" banner + tab auto-close for deliberately stopped sessions.
- `public/tasks-view.js:331-337` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî task delete (√ó) has neither confirm nor undo; archive has an undo toast, stop has a confirm dialog. At least add an undo toast.
- `public/session-tabs.js:126-133` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî tab close with `tabCloseBehavior=stopSession` kills the process without asking; the sidebar stop always confirms (`confirmAndStopSession`). Same destructive action, inconsistent pattern.
- `public/sidebar.js:1561` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî worktree-delete error uses native `alert()` ‚Äî the only place; everywhere else `showControlMessage`/`showControlDialog`.
- `public/viewer-panel.js:192,199,202` + `public/variables-admin.js:323` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî native `window.confirm`/`alert` for delete confirmation/errors while the rest of the app uses control dialogs/toasts.
- `public/control-dialogs.js:111-135` + `public/style.css:250` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî toasts do not stack: simultaneous `showControlToast` calls overlap pixel-exactly (fixed bottom/right), only the top one is readable. Fix: toast container with flex stacking.
- `public/viewer-panel.js:333-345` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî `_save()` has no catch and no error feedback: if `onSave` throws or returns `ok:false`, nothing happens for the user. Fix: catch + error flash/toast; success flash only on ok.
- `public/plans-memory-view.js:242-260` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî schedule play button shows checkmark + "Launched!" before `result.ok` is checked; errors go to `console.error` only; a rejected promise leaves the button stuck in the spinner. Fix: check result first, error branch with toast + icon reset.
- `public/handoff.js:321-325` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî `handoff-del`: a `deleteHandoff` failure is swallowed (`catch {}`) and the entry removed from the list anyway ‚Üí UI claims deleted although it failed; also no confirm before delete.
- `public/sidebar.js:1178-1182` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî `finalizeSidebar` focuses the active terminal after every re-render (i.e. every status tick); keyboard focus on sidebar buttons/headers is constantly stolen (a11y ‚Äî defeats `makeButtonLike`). The guard only checks inputs.
- `public/bookmarks-tags.js:456-461` + `public/tasks-view.js:344` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî jump/open passes a minimal `{ sessionId }` ‚Üí viewer title shows a raw UUID instead of the session name; try `sessionMap.get(sessionId)` first.
- `public/handoff.js:181-196` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `showHandoffCaptureBar` has no Escape handler and no way to dismiss except the two buttons; the promise/bar stays open forever when ignored.
- `public/project-sort.js:48` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `localeCompare(‚Ä¶, 'de')` hardcoded although the app is English throughout; slightly wrong alpha sort for non-German users. Fix: `undefined` (system locale).
- `public/sidebar.js:1416-1421` vs. `:1736-1741` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî project favorite toggle triggers a full `loadProjects()` (2√ó getProjects IPC) while session pin only does `refreshSidebar({resort:true})` ‚Äî inconsistent and needlessly expensive.

## Acceptance

- Each finding fixed or explicitly rejected (one-line reason in an issue comment).
- `npm test` green. UI strings in English, matching existing tone.

title: Review 2026-07: renderer performance ‚Äî full rebuilds, O(N¬≤) loops, per-keystroke waste
body:
# Renderer performance: full rebuilds, O(N¬≤) loops, per-keystroke waste

Cluster from the 2026-07-05 multi-agent code review. Line numbers pin to commit `2a10383`. **Verify every finding against the current code before fixing** ‚Äî reject non-reproducible findings with a one-line reason.

## Findings

- `public/app.js:381-386` + `public/sidebar.js:1189-1226` ‚Äî **high** ‚Äî every busy/idle/attention edge of ANY session (`setActivity` ‚Üí `refreshSessionStatusViews` ‚Üí `refreshSidebar`) rebuilds the complete sidebar (`buildSessionItem` for all sessions, morphdom diff, then `rebindSidebarEvents` with ~15 `querySelector` per item). Constant load with many sessions/agents. Fix: patch status classes/chips in place for status ticks (like `updateGridCardStatuses` does for the grid); full rebuild only for structural changes.
- `public/grid-view.js:611` ‚Äî **high** ‚Äî `wrapInGridCard` calls `updateRunningIndicators()` ‚Üí `updateGridCardStatuses()` iterating all cards, per card ‚áí O(N¬≤) on every grid rebuild (showGridView, filter click, status change with rebuild). Fix: call once after the render loop.
- `public/codemirror-setup.js:63` and `:112` ‚Äî **high** ‚Äî `doc.toLowerCase().indexOf(term, pos)` inside the while condition ‚Üí the whole document is re-lowercased per match (O(n¬∑matches), twice: highlighter + search bar, on every keystroke). Fix: `const lower = doc.toLowerCase()` once before the loop.
- `public/sidebar.js:349`, `:460`, `:817`, `:1029` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `getCollapsedGroups()`/`getExpandedSlugs()`/`getExpandedSubagents()`/`getProjectCollapseState()` do a `localStorage` read + `JSON.parse` per group/project per render ‚Äî cache once per render pass.
- `public/utils.js:40-44` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `escapeHtml` creates a `div` per call; in the row loops of projects-admin/variables-admin (~10 calls per row) needless DOM allocations. Fix: regex-replace variant.

## Acceptance

- Each finding fixed or explicitly rejected (one-line reason in an issue comment).
- `npm test` green; sidebar behavior unchanged (collapse state, selection, drag) after the in-place status patching.

title: Review 2026-07: terminal (xterm) polish ‚Äî debug leftovers, stale-tail flush, cleanups
body:
# Terminal (xterm) polish: debug leftovers, stale-tail flush, structural cleanups

Cluster from the 2026-07-05 code review (deep manual pass over `public/terminal-manager.js`). Line numbers pin to commit `2a10383`. **Verify every finding against the current code before fixing.**

Context: the draw pipeline is already advanced (30 fps write coalescing, background skip + replay buffer with 2 MB cap, LRU cap, per-view scrollback budgets, opt-in WebGL lifecycle, ConPTY `windowsPty` hints, `?2026` sync guard). These are the remaining gaps.

## Findings

- `public/terminal-manager.js:1051-1067` ‚Äî **high (trivial fix)** ‚Äî TEMP DEBUG F5 handler still in production; the comment itself says "REMOVE after diagnosis". It globally hijacks F5 (suppresses default). Remove.
- `public/terminal-manager.js:983/1013` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî `showSession` drains `rawReplayBuffers` but not a pending `terminalWriteBuffers` chunk. Background flush interval is 2000 ms ‚Üí after a tab switch the last output tail can lag up to 2 s. Fix: call `flushTerminalBuffer(sessionId)` before `drainReplayBuffer(sessionId)` in both branches.
- `public/terminal-manager.js:204-265` ‚Äî **low (structural)** ‚Äî the `isBottomRowClipped`/`safeFit` self-heal (30-frame retry, post-paint re-check) works around FitAddon's padding bug. Long-term cleaner: replace FitAddon's propose with a fully own row/col computation (the math already exists in `terminal-fit.js`), which removes the retry loop and both self-heal hooks. Larger change ‚Äî evaluate, don't rush.
- `public/terminal-manager.js:622` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `TERMINAL_LRU_CAP = 12` hardcoded ‚Äî candidate for a setting (users with much RAM / many parallel sessions).

## Acceptance

- Each finding fixed or explicitly rejected (one-line reason in an issue comment).
- `npm test` green. For the flush fix: manual check ‚Äî stream output in a background tab, switch to it, the tail must appear immediately (no ~2 s lag).

title: Terminal mouse mode: direct left-click selection with working TUI wheel scroll (PowerShell-style)
body:
# Feature proposal: direct left-click text selection while a TUI captures the mouse (decision pending)

Status: **proposal ‚Äî not yet decided whether/how to ship.** This issue documents the problem, two candidate solutions with trade-offs, and the research references, so the decision can be made (and implemented) in a later session without re-research.

## Problem

When a TUI (Claude Code) enables xterm mouse reporting (DEC private modes 1000/1002/1003/1006), xterm.js forwards mouse events to the program:

- Left-drag no longer selects text locally ‚Äî selection requires Shift+drag (Win/Linux) or Alt+drag (macOS with `macOptionClickForcesSelection`).
- The existing "Mouse reporting" setting (off = `stripMouseReporting`) restores left-click selection but kills wheel scrolling inside the TUI, because wheel reports are stripped too.
- PageUp/PageDown are keyboard input and are unaffected in every scenario.

Goal: left-click selects text directly AND the wheel still scrolls the TUI content ‚Äî the classic Windows console (conhost/PowerShell QuickEdit) feel.

Upstream xterm.js offers no official option; the custom-mouse-bindings request is open since 2018 and marked out-of-scope (see references).

## Option A ‚Äî patch `SelectionService.shouldForceSelection` (internal API)

Verified against the bundled `@xterm/xterm` 6.0.x: the method is

```js
shouldForceSelection(e){ return isMac ? e.altKey && macOptionClickForcesSelection : e.shiftKey }
```

Override per terminal instance in `createTerminalEntry` (public/terminal-manager.js):

```js
const svc = terminal._core._selectionService;
const orig = svc.shouldForceSelection.bind(svc);
svc.shouldForceSelection = (e) => e.button === 0 || orig(e);
```

Effect: plain left-drag always performs local selection (button events are then not reported to the program while selecting); wheel reports keep flowing to the TUI (separate path via CoreMouseService); keyboard untouched.

**Pros**
- Minimal code (~5 lines + setting).
- Wheel behavior stays 100% native (real SGR wheel reports ‚Äî exact TUI semantics).
- Copy works immediately via the existing Ctrl+C-with-selection handler.

**Cons**
- `_core` is internal API ‚Äî fragile across xterm upgrades; needs a guard (feature-detect the method, fall back gracefully) and a note in the upgrade checklist.
- The TUI loses left-click events entirely (Claude Code barely uses them, but e.g. clicking UI elements in other TUIs would stop working).
- Middle/right button behavior must be re-checked after the patch (right-click is already intercepted by our context menu).

## Option B ‚Äî conhost/PowerShell emulation via public API

Building blocks, all public:

1. Strip mouse reporting (existing setting/`stripMouseReporting`) ‚Üí left-click selects locally, exactly like conhost QuickEdit. While stripping, remember per session that the program *wanted* mouse mode (it currently just drops the sequences).
2. Right-click paste already exists (`terminalRightClick` mode `copy-paste` ‚Äî PuTTY/conhost style).
3. Missing piece ‚Äî wheel: `terminal.attachCustomWheelEventHandler(e => ‚Ä¶)` (public xterm API, present in the bundled 6.0.x). When the program had requested mouse mode, translate wheel ticks into key input to the PTY (e.g. `\x1b[5~`/`\x1b[6~` PageUp/PageDown, or 3√ó arrow up/down per tick) so the TUI scrolls as if the user pressed keys. When no mouse mode was requested (plain shell), do nothing ‚Üí wheel scrolls xterm scrollback locally as usual.

**Pros**
- Public API only ‚Äî survives xterm upgrades.
- Complete conhost feel: left-click select, right-click paste, wheel scrolls content, Bild‚Üë/‚Üì native.
- Conditional behavior (TUI vs. plain shell) ‚Äî plain shells keep local scrollback scrolling.

**Cons**
- Emulated keys ‚âÝ native wheel semantics: granularity must be tuned (1 tick = one PageUp is coarse; 3√ó arrow may be interpreted differently by the TUI ‚Äî Claude Code's exact key handling needs testing).
- More code (~30-40 lines: session flag while stripping, wheel handler, setting).
- Key choice is app-specific; other TUIs may expect different scroll keys.

## Recommendation (from the review session)

Option B as the shipped mode (robust, upgrade-safe), exposed as a third mouse setting, e.g. `Mouse mode: native | select (PowerShell-style)`, default `native`. Option A only as a fallback experiment if B's key emulation feels wrong in Claude Code.

## References

- https://github.com/xtermjs/xterm.js/issues/1536 (custom mouse bindings ‚Äî out of scope upstream)
- https://github.com/xtermjs/xterm.js/discussions/4320 (mouse reporting toggle discussion)
- https://github.com/xtermjs/xterm.js/issues/4329 (force-selection modifier differences)
- https://xtermjs.org/docs/api/terminal/interfaces/iterminaloptions/

## Acceptance (once approved)

- New setting wired through settings-panel + live apply (pattern: `window._applyTerminalMouseReporting`).
- Left-drag selects text while Claude Code runs; wheel scrolls Claude Code's transcript; PageUp/PageDown unchanged; plain shells keep local scrollback wheel behavior.
- `npm test` green; fork-feature documented in README "What this fork adds" + docs/fork-features.md.

title: Worktree/session history navigation
body:
## Requirement

**Worktree/session history navigation** _(Source: supacode)_ ‚Äî Hotkey (e.g. Alt+‚Üê/‚Üí) to step forward/back through the most recently selected sessions/projects (MRU stack). Fits the porting workflow with many worktrees/branches. Pure renderer nav over the existing session switch.

title: Per-session/project color + custom title
body:
## Requirement

**Per-session/project color + custom title** _(Source: supacode)_ ‚Äî freely selectable accent color per session/project row + optional short title for quick visual separation with many parallel sessions. Distinction: `customizing-colors.md` is global theming, here **per entry**. May use existing favorites/groups infrastructure.

title: "Dismiss All" for notifications
body:
## Requirement

**"Dismiss All" for notifications** _(Source: supacode)_ ‚Äî a button/action that acknowledges all open in-app notifications / attention markers at once. Complements the Attention inbox (#13). Small, pure renderer + possibly state reset.

title: Remote repo over SSH (treat like local)
body:
## Requirement

**Remote repo over SSH (treat like local)** _(Source: supacode)_ ‚Äî open a repo/project on a remote host via SSH and manage it in Switchboard like a local one: session scan, terminal (PTY over SSH), file/diff access. **Large** ‚Äî needs its own detailed plan (transport, auth via Bitwarden SSH agent, remote `~/.claude/projects` scan, PTY bridging, watcher replacement). Clarify scope/architecture before implementation.

title: Agent badges: which tool runs where (evaluate)
body:
## Requirement

**Agent badges: which tool runs where (evaluate)** _(source: supacode)_ ‚Äî per session/row a badge showing the **agent type** (Claude / Codex / Copilot CLI / OpenCode) + live presence, hook-driven. Partial redundancy with #13 (status chips, but Claude only). Only makes sense together with #42 (Multi-LLM). **First evaluate** whether standalone or folded into #13.

title: Multi-LLM / multi-agent support (Codex, ‚Ä¶)
body:
## Requirement

**Multi-LLM / multi-agent support (Codex, ‚Ä¶)** ‚Äî alongside Claude Code, launch & manage other coding CLIs as a session type: **Codex**, GitHub Copilot CLI, OpenCode, and others. Affects session start (command/args per agent), detection/scan (not just `~/.claude/projects`), possibly a dedicated session-cache source. **Large**, needs its own detailed plan; foundation for #41.

title: Detect subagent spawn via .meta.json (not only .jsonl)
body:
## Requirement

**Detect subagent spawn via `.meta.json` (not only `.jsonl`)** ‚Äî when spawning a task subagent, Claude Code immediately writes `<parent>/subagents/agent-<id>.meta.json` (`agentType`, `description`, `toolUseId`, `spawnDepth`), but the `.jsonl` transcript only once the agent produces output. A spawned agent that did not (yet) run to producing output (queued, parent turn ends/is interrupted) has **only** `meta.json` ‚Üí `detectSubagentTransitions`/`enumerateSessionFiles` (which only scan `subagents/*.jsonl`) never see it (observed 2026-07-02: session `876c2b28`, "Feldtexte W6" agents ‚Äî meta identical to runnable W5 agents, but without jsonl; parent last at 22:26, spawns at 22:28). **No special background-agents format, no data loss ‚Äî pure visibility.** Fix: also scan `subagents/*.meta.json` ‚Üí placeholder subagent row (name from `description`, status "spawned/empty/aborted") as soon as the meta is there; upgrade to a full row when the `.jsonl` arrives. Files: `read-session-file.js` (`enumerateSessionFiles`), `session-transitions.js`, `session-cache.js`, subagent rendering in `public/sidebar.js`.

title: Grid layout a11y + DOM tests
body:
## Requirement

**Grid layout a11y + DOM tests** ‚Äî bonus addition to #04 (code done, beyond the Spec-08 acceptance criteria). (a) **Keyboard operation** for card resize/reorder: currently only pointer drag (`startCardResize`/`startCardDrag`), no keyboard path; `navigateGrid` only moves focus, the resize handle is `aria-hidden`. To add: resize the focused card via `Shift`+arrow, a "move mode" for reordering, ARIA live announcements. File: `public/grid-view.js`. (b) **DOM/integration tests** for resize/drag/persistence/group-drop ‚Äî so far only the pure model is tested (`test/grid-layout.test.js`), the interaction path is untested; needs a jsdom or Electron E2E harness (the project runs `node --test` without a DOM).

title: HTML/MD preview panel (+ image preview)
body: ## Requirement **HTML/MD preview panel (+ image preview)** _(source: brianstanley)_ ‚Äî show rendered Markdown/HTML of a file as a preview. Renderer-side, reusing the existing `marked` + tag escape/sanitizing from the JSONL viewer. Ref: `brianstanley/main` @ `bbc9e52`. **When porting, watch out for:** (a) preserve the #46 DOMPurify wrap at both Markdown-innerHTML spots (the brianstanley diff writes raw `marked` back at `viewer-panel.js:397`); (b) sign off on HTML preview = sandboxed iframe (`allow-same-origin`, no `allow-scripts`). **Additional scope (from #48 test):** the file panel/`ViewerPanel` currently renders images as byte text (`read-file-for-panel` always reads UTF-8) ‚Äî add image extensions (`png/jpg/jpeg/gif/webp/svg`) as ``/data-URL preview; possibly a binary read path in the IPC. Files: `public/{viewer-panel,viewer-toolbar,file-panel}.js`, `style.css`, possibly `main.js`/`preload.js`.

title: Per-session API key override
body:
## Requirement

**Per-session API key override** _(split off from #47)_ ‚Äî optional password field in the session dialogs that injects `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` **only transiently** per session into the PTY `env` (`open-terminal`, `main.js`) ‚Äî **never** in the command string or the settings DB. Persistence only via `saved_variables` `secret` (`safeStorage`). Picker layer from `brianstanley` @ `fb81d18`. **Net-new** (we are OAuth/Claude-only) ‚Äî before implementing, clarify whether it's even wanted.

title: Open files in a configurable external editor
body:
Let users open files in their own external editor.

- A **configurable external editor** (command / path) in settings.
- In the integrated file panel, a toolbar button next to "Toggle line wrapping" that opens the current file in the external editor and **closes the panel**.
- In the terminal, **Ctrl/Cmd+click** on a file link opens it in the external editor; a plain click keeps opening the integrated panel.
- Fall back to the OS default application when no external editor is configured.

title: Review 2026-07: code consolidation ‚Äî duplicated logic and dead code
body:
# Code consolidation: duplicated logic and dead code

Cluster from the 2026-07-05 multi-agent code review. Line numbers pin to commit `2a10383`. **Verify every finding against the current code before fixing** ‚Äî reject non-reproducible findings with a one-line reason. Pure refactoring: no behavior change, each consolidation must keep tests green.

## Duplications

- `claude-config.js:74-189` ‚Äî `setProjectTrust` / `removeProjectEntry` / `renameProjectEntry`: 3√ó identical read‚Üíparse‚Üí`.bak`‚Üítmp‚Üírename block ‚Üí shared `mutateClaudeConfig(fn)`.
- `main.js:1977` / `:2566` / `:2253` ‚Äî settings cascade (default‚Üíglobal‚Üíproject) written out 3√ó (resolveShellTypeForProject, open-terminal IIFE, get-effective-settings) ‚Üí `effectiveSetting(key, projectPath)` helper.
- `main.js:2427` vs. `:2444` ‚Äî read-session-jsonl / read-subagent-jsonl: identical "file‚Üíentries[]" parse block ‚Üí shared `readJsonlEntries(path)`.
- `public/dialogs.js:235-327` vs. `:386-461` ‚Äî permission-mode grid (modes array, `renderModeGrid`, grid click handler, overlay/key wiring) duplicated 1:1 between New and Resume dialog ‚Üí shared `buildPermissionModeDialog()`.
- `public/dialogs.js:62-69`, `:196-203`, `public/app.js:1773-1780` ‚Äî "inject pending session into cachedProjects/cachedAllProjects" block 3√ó ‚Üí `injectPendingSession(session, projectPath)`.
- `public/jsonl-viewer.js:724-733`/`819-829`/`333-341` (toolResultMap) and `:757-767`/`:848-858`/`:495-503` (fullscreen overlay) ‚Äî transcript render logic duplicated 3√ó between `showJsonlViewer`, `showSubagentTranscript`, agent expand ‚Üí shared `renderTranscript(entries, container)`.
- `public/sidebar.js:2386-2479` vs. `:2484-2553` ‚Äî drag scaffold (threshold, ghost, moveGhost, cleanup, onUp) copied twice (session drag vs. project drag) ‚Üí generic `startPointerDrag(opts)`.
- `public/app.js:2101-2175` ‚Äî tab switch hides viewers per branch with 7+ hand-maintained `style.display='none'` lists (already diverged between branches) ‚Üí `hideAllViewers()` + show target explicitly.
- `public/app.js:2512-2519` vs. `public/settings-panel.js:1191-1195` ‚Äî usage-threshold clamping implemented twice with identical semantics ‚Üí one helper in utils.
- `workers/search-query.js:64,78-80` vs. `db.js:370,1040-1042` ‚Äî `FTS_QUERY_MAX_CHARS` + escape/`title:` logic duplicated ("must match" comment instead of a shared module) ‚Üí shared `search-query-util.js`.
- `shell-profiles.js:40-44` vs. `:128-133` ‚Äî Git-Bash candidate list maintained twice ‚Üí shared constant.
- `workers/scan-projects.js:10-23` vs. `session-cache.js:48-70` ‚Äî `readFolderFromFilesystem` (deriveProjectPath ‚Üí enumerate ‚Üí readSessionFile) exists twice; drift risk ‚Üí shared Electron-free helper.
- `public/codemirror-setup.js:56-68` vs. `:109-117` ‚Äî identical match-scan loop in `searchHighlighter` and `findAll()` ‚Üí shared `findMatches(doc, term)`.
- `public/terminal-manager.js:689-704` vs. `:727-734` ‚Äî `linkHandler.activate` and the WebLinksAddon callback carry the same logic (button guard + `file://` branch) ‚Üí one shared `activateLink(event, uri)`.

## Dead code / cleanup

- `read-session-file.js:336` ‚Äî `readSessionDisplayHeader` is unused (see also the sync-IO issue, where it should become used again ‚Äî coordinate).
- `public/jsonl-viewer.js:14-52`, `:285-301` ‚Äî dead live-tail infrastructure: `liveSubagents` only ever filled, `data-subagent-watch-key` never set, `activeWatchId` never assigned. Remove or finish the feature.
- `sqlite-busy-retry.js:22` ‚Äî unreachable `throw lastErr` (last attempt rethrows in the catch) ‚Üí remove.
- `derive-project-path.js:47` ‚Äî signature takes 1 argument but ‚â•6 call sites pass a second `folder` (main.js:873,1455,1638, session-cache.js:51,449,569, workers/scan-projects.js:12) ‚Äî dead argument suggests unused semantics ‚Üí clean call sites.
- `public/plans-memory-view.js:70-76` ‚Äî active marking via text comparison of all `.session-id` (breaks with same-named files) while `openMemory:273` correctly uses `data-filepath` + `CSS.escape` ‚Üí same data-attribute technique.
- `public/style.css` ‚Äî ~15 top-level selectors defined more than once (e.g. `.variable-secret-pill` 7664+7681, `.session-stop-btn` 2007+2071, `.grid-card` 2√ó); complementary today but collision-prone ‚Üí consolidate per selector.

## Acceptance

- Each item consolidated or explicitly rejected (one-line reason in an issue comment).
- `npm test` green; zero behavior change (pure refactor).

title: Review 2026-07: test gaps and build-script hardening
body:
# Test gaps and build-script hardening

Cluster from the 2026-07-05 multi-agent code review. Line numbers pin to commit `2a10383`. **Verify every finding against the current code before fixing.**

## Test gaps

- `schedule-ipc.js` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî entirely untested; especially the security-relevant `run-schedule-now` path validation (see the security-hardening issue) is pure logic and trivially testable once extracted into a function.
- `shell-profiles.js` ‚Äî **medium** ‚Äî `test/schedule-injection.test.js` covers only bash+pwsh quoting; the (buggy, see robustness issue) cmd branch, `resolveShell`, and `shellArgs` are untested.
- `encode-project-path.js` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî no test although the module must reproduce CLI behavior ("reverse-engineered from claude CLI 2.1.126"); the >200-char hash branch is regression-prone.
- `test/folder-index-state.test.js:18` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî real 1.1 s sleep to advance mtime ‚Äî slows the suite. Fix: `fs.utimesSync` with an explicit timestamp.
- `workers/scan-projects.js` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî no protocol test (progress/final/error message shape) although the search worker has one (`search-worker-protocol.test.js`); shape drift vs. the consumer would go unnoticed.

## Build scripts

- `scripts/generate-icons.js:117-131` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî final async IIFE without `.catch` ‚Üí on `loadImage` failure an unhandled rejection instead of exit code ‚âÝ 0 (build "succeeds" without Linux icons). Fix: `.catch(e => { console.error(e); process.exit(1); })`.
- `scripts/postinstall.js:28` + `scripts/generate-icons.js:63-72` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `execSync` with string-interpolated paths (project convention is `execFile`); breaks on paths with special chars; PIL is an undeclared build dependency for generate-icons. Fix: `execFileSync` with arg arrays; document the PIL requirement.
- `scripts/build-backlog.js:21` ‚Äî **low** ‚Äî `--limit 200`: with >200 open issues the mirror silently truncates. Fix: warn/fail at `issues.length === 200` or paginate.

## Acceptance

- Each finding fixed or explicitly rejected (one-line reason in an issue comment).
- New tests follow the existing `node --test` style in `test/`; `npm test` green.
